text.title: Cost Optimization Patterns
text.summary: Rules for prompt caching, Batch API usage, token counting pre-flight, and measuring actual savings in production Claude pipelines.

rule.statement: Place the most stable content at the top of the system prompt with cache_control: ephemeral. Put dynamic instructions last.
rule.rationale: Cache uses prefix matching. Dynamic content at the top busts the cache on every call. Stable content at the top maximizes the proportion of input tokens that are cached.

rule.statement: Never inject timestamps, request IDs, or user-specific values before the cached block.
rule.rationale: Even a single character difference in the prefix causes a cache miss. You pay the write cost (25% above regular) with no read benefit.

rule.statement: Measure cache_read_input_tokens / input_tokens ratio per pipeline run. Target above 0.70.
rule.rationale: Below 70% cache hit rate, write overhead starts eroding the 90% read savings. Low hit rate indicates the cached prefix is being modified per call.

rule.statement: Use Batch API for workloads that tolerate up to 24-hour latency to receive 50% cost reduction.
rule.rationale: Batch API cuts cost in half with no other code changes. The only requirement is that the workload is not real-time: offline enrichment, nightly analysis, evaluation suites.

rule.statement: Use client.messages.count_tokens() for pre-flight checks before paying for large-input calls.
rule.rationale: Token counting is cheap (no generation cost) and prevents paying full generation cost for inputs that exceed the context limit, which fail with an API error.

example.code:
import anthropic
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
# Cached system prompt structure STATIC_DOCS = "... large stable documentation (10k+ tokens) ..."
system = [ { "type": "text", "text": STATIC_DOCS, "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"} # cached prefix }, { "type": "text", "text": dynamic_instructions # not cached; appended per-run } ]
response = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=4096, system=system, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_task}] )
# Monitor cache effectiveness u = response.usage hit_rate = u.cache_read_input_tokens / max(u.input_tokens, 1)
example.note: Static docs are marked cached; dynamic instructions appended without cache_control.

text.reference: System prompt with static cached prefix and dynamic tail
